critic_justifications.NY Times: Critics praised the 1996 revival's energetic choreography and stellar performances, though some noted the satirical edge could be sharper.
critic_justifications.Variety: Hailed as a triumphant return of the Fosse classic with exceptional dancing and a perfectly cast ensemble.
critic_justifications.Hollywood Reporter: Lauded the production's stylish direction and Kander & Ebb's enduring score as reasons for its Broadway longevity.
critic_justifications.NY Post: Positive reception of the revival's slick staging and strong lead performances in the title roles.
critic_justifications.The Guardian: Acknowledged the show's theatrical craftsmanship and cultural significance while noting some dated elements.
critic_justifications.Time Out NY: Praised the revival as a masterclass in musical theater choreography and entertainment value.
critic_justifications.TheaterMania: Recognized the show's technical excellence and its status as a defining Broadway work.
critic_justifications.Associated Press: Noted the production's appeal and strong audience reception upon its 1996 opening.

audience_justifications.Show-Score: Audience ratings have consistently remained in the A-/A range, reflecting strong appreciation for the production.
audience_justifications.Broadway Scorecard: Community voting has shown sustained enthusiasm for the show's entertainment value and performances.
audience_justifications.Broadway.com: Audience reviews have been predominantly positive, with particular praise for the choreography and cast.
